papers.title_en: Hybrid Deep Learning with Temporal Data Augmentation for Accurate Remaining Useful Life Prediction of Lithium-Ion Batteries
papers.authors: Yun Tian, Guili Wang, Jian Bi, Kaixin Han, Chenglu Wu, Zhiyi Lu, Chenhao Li, Liangwang Sun, Minyu Zhou, Chenchen Xu
papers.evidence: 锂离子电池剩余寿命（RUL）预测
papers.tldr: 本研究针对锂离子电池剩余寿命（RUL）预测中复杂工况和数据稀缺的挑战，提出了一种名为CDFormer的混合深度学习模型。该模型集成卷积神经网络、深度残差收缩网络和Transformer编码器，以提取多尺度时序特征并建模局部与全局退化动力学。同时，引入包含高斯噪声、时间扭曲和重采样的复合数据增强策略以提升鲁棒性。实验证明，CDFormer在多个真实数据集上均优于现有基准模型，为电池健康管理提供了更精确可靠的预测方案。
papers.abstract_en: Accurate prediction of lithium-ion battery remaining useful life (RUL) is essential for reliable health monitoring and data-driven analysis of battery degradation. However, the robustness and generalization capabilities of existing RUL prediction models are significantly challenged by complex operating conditions and limited data availability. To address these limitations, this study proposes a hybrid deep learning model, CDFormer, which integrates convolutional neural networks, deep residual shrinkage networks, and Transformer encoders extract multiscale temporal features from battery measurement signals, including voltage, current, and capacity. This architecture enables the joint modeling of local and global degradation dynamics, effectively improving the accuracy of RUL prediction.To enhance predictive reliability, a composite temporal data augmentation strategy is proposed, incorporating Gaussian noise, time warping, and time resampling, explicitly accounting for measurement noise and variability. CDFormer is evaluated on two real-world datasets, with experimental results demonstrating its consistent superiority over conventional recurrent neural network-based and Transformer-based baselines across key metrics. By improving the reliability and predictive performance of RUL prediction from measurement data, CDFormer provides accurate and reliable forecasts, supporting effective battery health monitoring and data-driven maintenance strategies.

papers.title_en: Transformer-Based Prognostics: Enhancing Network Availability by Improved Monitoring of Optical Fiber Amplifiers
papers.authors: Dominic Schneider, Lutz Rapp, Christoph Ament
papers.evidence: 通过状态监测预测光纤放大器寿命
papers.tldr: 本研究旨在提高光网络的可用性和可靠性，提出了一种轻量级 Transformer 模型，通过基于状态的监控数据预测光纤放大器的寿命。该方法实现了实时、边缘级的预测性维护，推动了用于自主网络运行的可部署人工智能技术的发展，为光网络组件的健康管理提供了高效的解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: We enhance optical network availability and reliability through a lightweight transformer model that predicts optical fiber amplifier lifetime from condition-based monitoring data, enabling real-time, edge-level predictive maintenance and advancing deployable AI for autonomous network operation.

papers.title_en: Self-Supervised Learning for Knee Osteoarthritis: Diagnostic Limitations and Prognostic Value of Hospital Data
papers.authors: Haresh Rengaraj Rajamohan, Yuxuan Chen, Kyunghyun Cho, Cem M. Deniz
papers.evidence: 用于联合诊断和预后的自监督学习
papers.tldr: 本研究评估了自监督学习（SSL）在膝骨关节炎（OA）诊断和预后建模中的效果。通过对比仅图像SSL和结合放射科报告的图文多模态SSL，发现SSL在KL等级诊断任务中表现平平，但在预测4年结构性进展等预后任务中显著优于ImageNet预训练模型。研究揭示了预训练数据分布与下游任务对齐的重要性，为医疗影像AI的预训练策略提供了新见解。
papers.abstract_en: This study assesses whether self-supervised learning (SSL) improves knee osteoarthritis (OA) modeling for diagnosis and prognosis relative to ImageNet-pretrained initialization. We compared (i) image-only SSL pretrained on knee radiographs from the OAI, MOST, and NYU cohorts, and (ii) multimodal image-text SSL pretrained on hospital knee radiographs paired with radiologist impressions. For diagnostic Kellgren-Lawrence (KL) grade prediction, SSL yielded mixed results. While image-only SSL improved accuracy during linear probing (frozen encoder), it did not outperform ImageNet pretraining during full fine-tuning. Similarly, multimodal SSL failed to improve grading performance. A likely explanation is mismatch between the hospital pretraining corpus and the downstream diagnostic task: the hospital image-text dataset was restricted to knees from patients with clinically identified OA in routine care, rather than a cohort spanning the full spectrum from normal to severe disease needed for balanced KL grading. In addition, radiology impressions do not explicitly encode KL grade, limiting supervision for learning KL-specific decision boundaries. In contrast, this same multimodal initialization significantly improved prognostic modeling. It outperformed ImageNet baselines in predicting 4-year structural incidence and progression, including on external validation (MOST AUROC: 0.701 vs. 0.599 at 10\% labeled data). Overall, these results suggest that our hospital image-text data may be less effective for diagnostic grading when the pretraining cohort is limited to OA knees, but can provide a strong signal for prognostic modeling when the downstream task is better aligned with the pretraining data distribution.

papers.title_en: AdaLTM: Adaptive Layer-wise Task Vector Merging for Categorical Speech Emotion Recognition with ASR Knowledge Integration
papers.authors: Chia-Yu Lee, Huang-Cheng Chou, Tzu-Quan Lin, Yuanchao Li, Ya-Tse Wu, Shrikanth Narayanan, Chi-Chun Lee
papers.evidence: 多任务学习与任务向量合并
papers.tldr: 本研究针对语音情感识别（SER）中集成自动语音识别（ASR）知识时存在的优化冲突问题，提出了AdaLTM框架。该方法基于WavLM-Large模型，通过提取特定任务的向量并利用逐层可学习系数将其合并到冻结的基座模型中。这种深度感知的平衡策略有效融合了语言与副语言信息，在MSP-Podcast数据集上证明了其能显著缓解任务间干扰，提升情感识别性能。
papers.abstract_en: Integrating Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) into Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) enhances modeling by providing linguistic context. However, conventional feature fusion faces performance bottlenecks, and multi-task learning often suffers from optimization conflicts. While task vectors and model merging have addressed such conflicts in NLP and CV, their potential in speech tasks remains largely unexplored. In this work, we propose an Adaptive Layer-wise Task Vector Merging (AdaLTM) framework based on WavLM-Large. Instead of joint optimization, we extract task vectors from in-domain ASR and SER models fine-tuned on emotion datasets. These vectors are integrated into a frozen base model using layer-wise learnable coefficients. This strategy enables depth-aware balancing of linguistic and paralinguistic knowledge across transformer layers without gradient interference. Experiments on the MSP-Podcast demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively mitigates conflicts between ASR and SER.

papers.title_en: Missing-Aware Multimodal Fusion for Unified Microservice Incident Management
papers.authors: Wenzhuo Qian, Hailiang Zhao, Ziqi Wang, Zhipeng Gao, Jiayi Chen, Zhiwei Ling, Shuiguang Deng
papers.evidence: 事件管理与异常检测的联合优化
papers.tldr: 针对微服务故障管理中多模态数据（指标、日志、追踪）常因网络波动而缺失的问题，本文提出ARMOR框架。该框架通过非对称编码器隔离模态差异，并利用缺失感知的门控融合机制与可学习占位符来减少干扰。ARMOR采用自监督自回归学习，在异常检测、故障分类和根因定位任务中均表现优异，即使在严重数据缺失下也能保持高诊断精度。
papers.abstract_en: Automated incident management is critical for microservice reliability. While recent unified frameworks leverage multimodal data for joint optimization, they unrealistically assume perfect data completeness. In practice, network fluctuations and agent failures frequently cause missing modalities. Existing approaches relying on static placeholders introduce imputation noise that masks anomalies and degrades performance. To address this, we propose ARMOR, a robust self-supervised framework designed for missing modality scenarios. ARMOR features: (i) a modality-specific asymmetric encoder that isolates distribution disparities among metrics, logs, and traces; and (ii) a missing-aware gated fusion mechanism utilizing learnable placeholders and dynamic bias compensation to prevent cross-modal interference from incomplete inputs. By employing self-supervised auto-regression with mask-guided reconstruction, ARMOR jointly optimizes anomaly detection (AD), failure triage (FT), and root cause localization (RCL). AD and RCL require no fault labels, while FT relies solely on failure-type annotations for the downstream classifier. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ARMOR achieves state-of-the-art performance under complete data conditions and maintains robust diagnostic accuracy even with severe modality loss.

papers.title_en: Missing-Aware Multimodal Fusion for Unified Microservice Incident Management
papers.authors: Wenzhuo Qian, Hailiang Zhao, Ziqi Wang, Zhipeng Gao, Jiayi Chen, Zhiwei Ling, Shuiguang Deng
papers.evidence: 事件管理联合优化的统一框架
papers.tldr: 针对微服务故障管理中多模态数据（指标、日志、追踪）经常缺失的问题，本文提出ARMOR框架。该框架通过非对称编码器隔离分布差异，并利用缺失感知的门控融合机制和可学习占位符来减少缺失数据带来的干扰。ARMOR采用自监督学习，在异常检测、故障分类和根因定位任务上均表现出色，即使在严重数据缺失下也能保持高诊断精度。
papers.abstract_en: Automated incident management is critical for microservice reliability. While recent unified frameworks leverage multimodal data for joint optimization, they unrealistically assume perfect data completeness. In practice, network fluctuations and agent failures frequently cause missing modalities. Existing approaches relying on static placeholders introduce imputation noise that masks anomalies and degrades performance. To address this, we propose ARMOR, a robust self-supervised framework designed for missing modality scenarios. ARMOR features: (i) a modality-specific asymmetric encoder that isolates distribution disparities among metrics, logs, and traces; and (ii) a missing-aware gated fusion mechanism utilizing learnable placeholders and dynamic bias compensation to prevent cross-modal interference from incomplete inputs. By employing self-supervised auto-regression with mask-guided reconstruction, ARMOR jointly optimizes anomaly detection (AD), failure triage (FT), and root cause localization (RCL). AD and RCL require no fault labels, while FT relies solely on failure-type annotations for the downstream classifier. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ARMOR achieves state-of-the-art performance under complete data conditions and maintains robust diagnostic accuracy even with severe modality loss.

papers.title_en: Data-Driven Probabilistic Fault Detection and Identification via Density Flow Matching
papers.authors: Joshua D. Ibrahim, Mahdi Taheri, Soon-Jo Chung, Fred Y. Hadaegh
papers.tldr: 针对非线性控制系统的执行器与传感器故障诊断问题，本文提出一种基于密度流匹配的概率故障检测与识别（FDI）方法。该方法利用2-Wasserstein度量刻画故障可检测性，并通过随机收缩分析推导分布分离上界。通过条件流匹配学习不同故障下的神经向量场，并结合FiLM技术实现连续故障幅值的泛化。在航天器姿态控制系统上的实验表明，该方法在故障区分度和降低误报率方面优于传统的扩展卡尔曼滤波（EKF）。
papers.abstract_en: Fault detection and identification (FDI) is critical for maintaining the safety and reliability of systems subject to actuator and sensor faults. In this paper, the problem of FDI for nonlinear control-affine systems under simultaneous actuator and sensor faults is studied. We model fault signatures through the evolution of the probability density flow along the trajectory and characterize detectability using the 2-Wasserstein metric. In order to introduce quantifiable guarantees for fault detectability based on system parameters and fault magnitudes, we derive upper bounds on the distributional separation between nominal and faulty dynamics. The latter is achieved through a stochastic contraction analysis of probability distributions in the 2-Wasserstein metric. A data-driven FDI method is developed by means of a conditional flow-matching scheme that learns neural vector fields governing density propagation under different fault profiles. To generalize the data-driven FDI method across continuous fault magnitudes, Gaussian bridge interpolation and Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) conditioning are incorporated. The effectiveness of our proposed method is illustrated on a spacecraft attitude control system, and its performance is compared with an augmented Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) baseline. The results confirm that trajectory-based distributional analysis provides improved discrimination between fault scenarios and enables reliable data-driven FDI with a lower false alarm rate compared with the augmented EKF.

papers.title_en: Physics-Embedded Feature Learning for AI in Medical Imaging
papers.authors: Pulock Das, Al Amin, Kamrul Hasan, Rohan Thompson, Azubike D. Okpalaeze, Liang Hong
papers.evidence: 联合多类分类与生长动力学建模
papers.tldr: 针对医疗影像中深度学习模型缺乏物理一致性和可解释性的问题，本文提出PhysNet框架。该框架将肿瘤生长的反应扩散物理模型直接嵌入ResNet的中间特征学习过程中，而非仅在输出层施加约束。通过端到端训练，PhysNet在实现高精度肿瘤分类的同时，能学习肿瘤密度场及扩散率、生长率等生物物理参数。在脑部MRI数据集上的实验表明，该方法在分类性能上优于多种主流模型，并提供了符合医学常识的可解释性。
papers.abstract_en: Deep learning (DL) models have achieved strong performance in an intelligence healthcare setting, yet most existing approaches operate as black boxes and ignore the physical processes that govern tumor growth, limiting interpretability, robustness, and clinical trust. To address this limitation, we propose PhysNet, a physics-embedded DL framework that integrates tumor growth dynamics directly into the feature learning process of a convolutional neural network (CNN). Unlike conventional physics-informed methods that impose physical constraints only at the output level, PhysNet embeds a reaction diffusion model of tumor growth within intermediate feature representations of a ResNet backbone. The architecture jointly performs multi-class tumor classification while learning a latent tumor density field, its temporal evolution, and biologically meaningful physical parameters, including tumor diffusion and growth rates, through end-to-end training. This design is necessary because purely data-driven models, even when highly accurate or ensemble-based, cannot guarantee physically consistent predictions or provide insight into tumor behavior. Experimental results on a large brain MRI dataset demonstrate that PhysNet outperforms multiple state-of-the-art DL baselines, including MobileNetV2, VGG16, VGG19, and ensemble models, achieving superior classification accuracy and F1-score. In addition to improved performance, PhysNet produces interpretable latent representations and learned bio-physical parameters that align with established medical knowledge, highlighting physics-embedded representation learning as a practical pathway toward more trustworthy and clinically meaningful medical AI systems.
